Through "total quantity control, quota allocation, appropriate units, and household management", the goal of "how much soup to soak how much steamed bread" has been achieved.

  With the "tightening curse" of water indicators in mind, the development of all regions and industries in Ningxia must be based on water resources. The means of water management, water use and the realization of the value of water ecological products have all undergone significant changes.

  "The size of the gate opening and the amount of water flowing can all be remotely controlled on the computer, making farmland irrigation more accurate and efficient." In Gaozha Town, Liton District, Wuzhong City, Mei Nanwei, chairman of the Minxin Agricultural Irrigation Service Professional Cooperative, witnessed the change of water management methods from "relying on experience" in the past to "relying on technology" now.

  The reason for being so careful with water use is that the water quota saved can be sold for money. Through efficient water-saving transformation, the average water consumption per mu of cultivated land in Litong District has dropped by 15%. The 8 million cubic meters of water rights saved were sold to Wuzhong Taiyangshan Industrial Park for 80 million yuan over 10 years, solving the urgent problem of the park's new projects having no water rights quota. In this way, the Yellow River water "flows" from low-yield agriculture to high-value industry, and every drop of the Yellow River water bursts out with greater productivity.

  ——Be thrifty and tighten the water-saving “tap”.

  Saving water means expanding development space, and water users should consciously try every means to save water.

  Ningxia Baofeng Energy Group Co., Ltd. is a large water user, with 38 million cubic meters of water rights. "This 38 million cubic meters of water rights is the red line. If it is not enough, we either buy water rights or save it ourselves." Chen Ming, the company's water affairs director, said that the company has achieved full-process management of water resources "taking, using, saving, and draining" through industrial coupling, differentiated utilization and process innovation, and has tied water-saving indicators to performance, guiding all employees to form a conscious awareness of water conservation. Last year, the company's water reuse rate reached 98.5%, and the unit product water consumption was reduced by more than 27% compared with the regional advanced value.

  The development and utilization of unconventional water sources such as recycled water has further increased water conservation in Ningxia. In Yanchi County, Wuzhong City, the complete recycling chain of recycled water, namely "wastewater treatment - reclaimed water upgrading - wetland purification - rainwater collection - reservoir regulation - comprehensive utilization", has achieved full collection and zero discharge of sewage in the county.

  "By exploring and promoting the development and utilization of recycled water, we have completed the replacement of irrigation water sources for 37,000 mu of ecological protection forest in the north of the city and 19,000 mu of urban greening, and solved the water needs of key industrial enterprises and the replenishment of the Yinmahu water system." Zhang Hongkun, deputy director of the Yanchang County Water Affairs Bureau, said that the local urban recycled water utilization rate has reached more than 80%, which can stably save 4 million cubic meters of fresh water each year and increase efficiency by 10 million yuan.

  The "Four Waters and Four Determinations" have stimulated the awareness of water conservation in all walks of life, including agricultural water conservation and efficiency increase, industrial water conservation and emission reduction, urban water conservation and loss reduction, and recycled water recycling. In the past five years, Ningxia has reduced the rice planting area by 549,000 mu, and the proportion of agricultural water use has dropped by 3.5 percentage points; the water consumption per 10,000 yuan of industrial added value in 51% of the industrial categories in the region has decreased, and the reuse rate of industrial water has reached 97.3%.



  A view of Diannong River in Yinchuan City, Ningxia (Photo taken on June 23, 2024) Photo by Wang Peng/This magazine

  Activate Green Gold

  A good ecology is the most universal welfare for the people and a strong driving force for promoting high-quality development. Aiming to transform the increasingly beautiful ecological "appearance" into the industrial "value" that enriches the people and increases their income, Ningxia has priced ecological products through institutional and mechanism reforms and promoted market-based transactions, transforming the traditional resource disadvantages, location disadvantages and environmental disadvantages into advantages for high-quality development and activating the green gold.

  ——Market pricing of resources and environmental factors, and the “six rights” reform will help price and trade all categories of natural resources.

  Last September, a special transaction took place at the foot of Liupan Mountain. As the carbon sink transferor of the soil and water conservation project, Guyuan City Pengyang County Shengze Water Investment Co., Ltd. and Ningxia Yahao New Energy Technology Co., Ltd. signed an agreement to trade 36,000 tons of soil and water conservation carbon sinks for a total amount of 1.116 million yuan.

  This is the first signed carbon sink transaction for the Yellow River Basin soil and water conservation project. The transaction proceeds will be fed back into the construction of Pengyang County's soil and water conservation and ecological protection and restoration projects.

  "The good mountains and water created by soil and water conservation can not only grow trees, grass and crops, but now they can also 'grow' money." Chao Yongfu, director of the Pengyang County Soil and Water Conservation Station, said that the carbon sink project for this transaction is located in the Licha small watershed in Wangwa Town, Pengyang County, where comprehensive soil and water loss control has been carried out continuously since 2000. According to calculations and verifications, the total amount of soil and water conservation carbon sinks in the watershed from 2002 to 2023 is 93,000 tons, and the value conversion amount of soil and water conservation ecological products can reach more than 3 million yuan.

  This transaction is a microcosm of Ningxia's "six rights" reform. Starting from 2021, Ningxia has successively launched the "six rights" reform of water rights, land rights, forest rights, pollution discharge rights, energy use rights, and carbon emission rights. By building a market system with proper rights confirmation, effective rights and powers, reasonable pricing, and orderly market entry, the key resource and environmental factors that once restricted Ningxia's economic development have been given commodity attributes, market attributes, and financial attributes. With the help of the "invisible hand" of the market, they are circulated, focusing on efficient industries and high-quality production capacity, forcing industries to transform to low-consumption and low-pollution methods.

  As of the end of June 2024, Ningxia has completed a total of 4,705 "six rights" transactions worth more than 34.6 billion yuan and mortgage financing of 4.34 billion yuan.

  At the beginning of this year, Ningxia issued the "Implementation Opinions on Accelerating the Comprehensive Reform of the "Six Rights" and Comprehensively Improving the Efficiency of Coordinated Allocation of Factors", further proposing to establish and improve a mechanism for intensive and coordinated development and utilization of resource and environmental factors, guide all kinds of factors to converge toward green and low-carbon industries and advanced productive forces, and broaden new paths for transforming ecological advantages into development advantages.

  ——Change the mindset and seek development, and turn the “disadvantages” of ecological location into “advantages” of high-quality development according to local conditions.

  Turning green mountains and clear waters into gold and silver requires the support of reform and a change in concepts. Ningxia has given full play to its subjective initiative and forged a long board for high-quality development based on its ecological location characteristics.

  Based on the characteristics, we dig out "selling points" and create a unique cultural tourism brand. In Ningxia, the mountains and valleys are deep, so they sell "cool climate". The "18℃ summer" cultural tourism brand in the southern mountainous area has gradually become popular and has become a popular destination for summer vacations in the country every year; surrounded by sand on three sides, they sell "vast sea and desolation". Desert tourism has become Ningxia's golden signboard. Zhongwei City, known as the "Desert Water City", received nearly 6 million tourists in the desert tourism sector in May this year alone...

  Technology helps turn governance into use, and resources are everywhere. With the help of technology, the saline-alkali land in the Ningxia Plain Irrigation Area has become a "golden and silver beach". Helan County, Yinchuan City, "uses fishing to control alkali" to activate the potential of land. Through the development and governance of a large area of saline-alkali land in the Yellow River Irrigation Area, it has built the largest white shrimp facility breeding cluster base in the northwest.

  "The water quality in saline-alkali land contains a certain salinity. With the help of the shrimp freshwater farming technology, whiteleg shrimp can quickly adapt to the improved breeding water. At the same time, whiteleg shrimp can also take away a large amount of salt and alkali from the original saline-alkali water, killing two birds with one stone." said Tang Liang, director of the Fisheries and Fishery Administration Bureau of the Ningxia Agriculture and Rural Affairs Department.

  Following the successful farming of whiteleg shrimp and large yellow croaker in saline-alkali water, many counties and districts in Ningxia have regarded saline-alkali land as a new type of resource, planned the direction of management from the perspective of the industrial chain, and successively carried out "land farming of seafood" such as blue crabs, yellowfin snapper, grouper, and Atlantic salmon. More and more saline-alkali lands have become rich fields.

  The main responsible person of the Ningxia Hui Autonomous Region Party Committee said that Ningxia has always adhered to ecological priority and green development, and consciously shouldered the important mission of maintaining ecological security in Northwest China and even the whole country. Green waters and green mountains are gold and silver mountains, which has become a common consensus and universal welfare of people of all ethnic groups in Ningxia. The "Jiangnan on the Great Wall" will surely become more and more beautiful.
translation_improvement_points:
1. The phrase "保护黄河安澜" (bǎohù Huánghé ānlán) is translated as "to protect the Yellow River." This misses the nuance of "安澜" (ānlán), which specifically means 'peaceful waves' or 'safety from flooding.' A more precise translation would be "to protect the Yellow River's tranquility and safety," emphasizing the goal of preventing disaster.
2. The translation of "宁夏作为唯一全境属于黄河流域的省区" as "Ningxia is the only province in the Yellow River Basin" omits the key detail of "全境" (quán jìng), which means "entire territory." A more accurate rendering is "Ningxia, as the only provincial-level region whose entire territory lies within the Yellow River Basin..."
3. The policy term "动态‘清零’" (dòngtài 'qīnglíng'), translated as "dynamically 'cleared,'" could be clarified. This term implies a continuous process of proactive elimination. A better phrasing would be "maintained at a 'dynamic zero' status" to convey the ongoing nature of the effort.
4. The phrase "黄河干流宁夏段水质连续8年保持Ⅱ类进出" is translated without the crucial detail "进出" (jìnchū), meaning "entering and exiting." A more complete translation is: "The water quality of the Yellow River's main stream has been maintained at Class II as it enters and exits Ningxia for eight consecutive years," highlighting that Ningxia does not degrade the water quality.
5. The colloquial saying "有多少汤泡多少馍" (yǒu duōshao tāng pào duōshao mó), literally translated as "how much soup to soak how much steamed bread," is culturally specific and awkward in English. A more functional, meaning-based translation would be to use an English equivalent like "cutting one's coat according to one's cloth" or to state the principle directly, such as "ensuring consumption is strictly limited by available resources."
6. The term "紧箍咒" (jǐngūzhòu), a reference to the Monkey King's magical constraining headband, is translated as a "tightening curse." This is creative but may be unclear. A more accessible translation would be "binding spell" or, more plainly, "a powerful constraint," to better convey the idea of an unavoidable and strict limitation.
7. The idiom "锱铢必较" (zīzhū bìjiào) is translated as "being so careful," which significantly understates its intensity. The idiom means to haggle over every tiny amount. A more faithful translation would be "scrutinizing every drop" or "being meticulous about every unit of water used."
8. The phrase "节水开源" (jiéshuǐ kāiyuán) is translated as "increased water conservation," which only captures the first half of the meaning. "节水" means saving water, while "开源" means opening up new sources. A better translation is "both conserve water and tap new sources."
9. The modern slang term "颜值" (yánzhí), meaning physical attractiveness, is translated as "appearance." This is too bland. A translation like "aesthetic appeal" or even the literal "'face value'" would better capture the contemporary and stylish tone of the original.
10. The term "长板" (chángbǎn), translated as "long board," is confusing without the context of Chinese 'barrel theory' where it represents a strength. It should be translated based on its meaning, such as "key advantages" or "strengths."
11. The phrase "变治为用" (biàn zhì wèi yòng) is translated as "turn governance into use," which is overly abstract. The context is turning remediated saline-alkali land into a usable resource. A clearer translation is "turning a liability into an asset" or "transforming remediation into utilization."
12. The title "点活绿之金" (diǎn huó lǜ zhī jīn) is translated as "Activate Green Gold." The term "点活" (diǎn huó) is more vivid, suggesting 'bringing to life' or 'igniting.' A more evocative translation would be "Bringing Green Gold to Life."
13. The description of scenery "眼之所及水面开阔、芦草丰美、鸥鸟盘旋、鱼潜水底" is translated functionally but loses the poetic, parallel structure of the original four-character phrases. A translation that better preserves the rhythm could be: "As far as the eye can see: vast waters, lush grasses, circling gulls, and fish diving deep."
14. The idiom "面貌也因之一新" (miànmào yě yīn zhī yī xīn) is translated as "appearance... has also been changed," which is weak. The idiom implies a complete transformation. A better phrasing is "has taken on a completely new look" or "has been utterly transformed."
15. The term "倒逼" (dàobī) is translated as "forcing." While correct, this key policy term implies compelling change through structural constraints. A more nuanced option could be "compelling a transformation" to better reflect this indirect but powerful pressure.
16. The nickname "塞上江南" (Sàishàng Jiāngnán) is translated as "Jiangnan on the Great Wall." This is a reasonable explanation, but an alternative that better captures the 'oasis' concept is "the Oasis beyond the Frontier," as 'Sài' (塞) refers to the frontier regions.
17. The phrase "致富田" (zhìfù tián) is translated as "rich fields." This is ambiguous, as it could mean rich in nutrients. The term specifically means fields that generate wealth. A more precise translation would be "fields of prosperity" or "wealth-generating fields."

original_text:
新华社太原7月11日电 题：新闻战士何云：最后一颗子弹留给自己

　　新华社记者万倩仪

　　巍巍太行，苍山如刃。在山西省左权县麻田村的西山上，太行新闻烈士纪念碑面向东方。纪念碑侧面镌刻着陆定一同志的题词：“一九四二年五月，华北新华日报社社长何云等四十余位同志壮烈牺牲，烈士们永垂不朽。”何云，这位以笔为枪的新闻战士，在1942年日军大扫荡中壮烈牺牲，年仅37岁。

　　何云1905年出生于浙江上虞县朱巷乡（今绍兴市上虞区永和镇），1933年，因在上海组织抗议日寇进攻华北的群众大游行，被国民党逮捕。4年的监狱生涯摧残了他的身躯，两条腿由于长年戴着脚镣，走起路来已经有些不便。饱经磨难后，何云的革命信仰却更为坚定，1937年恢复自由后，他毅然奔赴华北抗日前线。

　　1939年元旦，《新华日报》（华北版）创刊号出版，何云被任命为社长兼总编辑。在发刊词中，他鲜明提出：“《新华日报》华北分馆任务有三：一是立足华北，坚持敌后抗战，鼓励、推动全国团结抗战及进步；二是创建、巩固和扩大华北抗日根据地；三是团结华北文化战士，开展敌后文化运动与敌苦斗到底。”1941年，新华社华北总分社成立，何云兼任总分社社长。

　　夜深了，油灯下，一个瘦弱的身影还在工作，手不停地挥动着毛笔。这是吴青记忆中的何云——她的丈夫，一个总是伏案工作的人。在“百团大战”期间，何云曾随八路军总部和129师奔赴前线，主持战地新闻采访工作。在炮火纷飞的前线，稿件一经完成，即审即刻、即校即印，以最快的速度将“百团大战”取得的战绩传播至民间，鼓舞广大民众的抗战热情。

　　1942年5月下旬开始，日军调集3万兵力对太行根据地展开“铁壁合围”。“我们陷入敌人合击圈，被重重包围，当时六架敌机擦着山头低飞，疯狂轰炸扫射。重要山头都被敌人火力封锁，机枪声如骤雨一般。”时任《新华日报》（华北版）记者李庄在一篇回忆文章中这样描述当时的场景。

　　面对此情形，何云带领200多名报社人员紧急向庄子岭一带转移。他召集大家开会，会议决定化整为零，分头行动突围。何云带领几位同志转移到辽县东南大羊角村附近，架起电台，抄收延安新华社电讯。

　　5月28日黎明，何云和几位同志在大羊角村附近的山坡上被敌人发现。面对绝境，他冷静地对身边的战友说：“不要把子弹打光，留下最后两颗，一颗打我，一颗打你自己，我们绝不能活着当俘虏。”这时，一颗子弹射来，何云被击中，身负重伤。当医护人员赶来时，他挣扎着说：“我的伤不重，快去抢救倒在那边的同志吧。”这是他留下的最后一句话。

　　何云牺牲后，刘伯承将军痛惜道：“实在可惜啊！一武（左权）一文（何云），两员大将，为国捐躯了。”杨尚昆同志在《悼何云》中写道，“何云正在壮年，党所给予的任务尚待竭力完成，今竟不幸牺牲，这确是一个损失！”

　　今年5月，麻田八路军总部纪念馆迎来了络绎不绝的研学团队，每天都有上百名来自山西、河北、河南的学生前来瞻仰太行新闻烈士纪念碑，参观新闻烈士展厅。讲解员杨振宇说：“每每讲完何云的故事，展厅总会陷入一片肃穆的寂静，不少人都忍不住落泪。”

　　一位参观者的分享让杨振宇至今难忘，“正是那份对抗战必胜的坚定信念，让他在绝境中勇敢面对死亡。”何云与战友们用生命诠释的，远不止是党的新闻工作者的职业坚守，更是中华儿女在民族危亡之际的家国大义，这在中华民族抗战的壮阔画卷中，刻下了永不磨灭的精神印记。
english_translation:
Xinhua News Agency, Taiyuan, July 11 , Title: News Warrior He Yun: Save the last bullet for yourself

　　Xinhua News Agency reporter Wan Qianyi

　　The towering Taihang Mountains are like blades. On the west mountain of Matan Village, Zuoquan County, Shanxi Province, the Taihang Journalism Martyrs Monument faces east. On the side of the monument is engraved the inscription of Comrade Lu Dingyi: "In May 1942, He Yun, the president of the North China Xinhua Daily, and more than 40 comrades died heroically. The martyrs will be immortal." He Yun, a journalist who used his pen as a gun, died heroically in the Japanese army's sweep in 1942 at the age of 37.

　　He Yun was born in Zhuxiang Township, Shangyu County, Zhejiang Province (now Yonghe Town, Shangyu District, Shaoxing City) in 1905. In 1933, he was arrested by the Kuomintang for organizing a mass demonstration in Shanghai to protest against the Japanese invasion of North China. Four years in prison destroyed his body, and his legs were shackled for many years, making it difficult for him to walk. After suffering so much, He Yun's revolutionary beliefs became more firm. After regaining his freedom in 1937, he resolutely went to the anti-Japanese front in North China.

　　On New Year's Day 1939, the first issue of Xinhua Daily (North China Edition) was published, and He Yun was appointed president and editor-in-chief. In the introductory remarks, he clearly stated: "The North China Branch of Xinhua Daily has three tasks: first, to be based in North China, persist in the war of resistance behind enemy lines, encourage and promote national unity in the war of resistance and progress; second, to establish, consolidate and expand the anti-Japanese base area in North China; third, to unite cultural fighters in North China, carry out cultural movements behind enemy lines and fight the enemy to the end." In 1941, the North China General Branch of Xinhua News Agency was established, and He Yun concurrently served as the president of the General Branch.

　　Late at night, under the oil lamp, a thin figure is still working, waving his brush non-stop. This is He Yun in Wu Qing's memory - her husband, a man who always works at his desk. During the "Hundred Regiments Campaign", He Yun went to the front line with the Eighth Route Army Headquarters and the 129th Division to preside over battlefield news interviews. On the front line of artillery fire, once the manuscript was completed, it was immediately reviewed, proofread and printed, spreading the achievements of the "Hundred Regiments Campaign" to the people at the fastest speed, inspiring the enthusiasm of the general public for the war of resistance.

　　Starting from late May 1942, the Japanese army mobilized 30,000 troops to launch an "iron wall encirclement" against the Taihang base. "We were trapped in the enemy's attack circle and surrounded. At that time, six enemy planes flew low over the hills, bombing and strafing crazily. The important hills were blocked by enemy firepower, and the sound of machine guns was like a torrential rain." Li Zhuang, a reporter for the Xinhua Daily (North China Edition) at the time, described the scene in an article he recalled.

　　Faced with this situation, He Yun led more than 200 newspaper staff to urgently move to the Zhuangziling area. He called everyone to a meeting, and the meeting decided to break out of the encirclement by splitting up into smaller groups. He Yun led several comrades to move to the vicinity of Dayangjiao Village in the southeast of Liao County, set up a radio station, and copied the Xinhua News Agency telegrams from Yan'an.

　　At dawn on May 28, He Yun and several comrades were discovered by the enemy on a hillside near Dayangjiao Village. Faced with a desperate situation, he calmly said to his comrades around him: "Don't use up all your bullets. Save the last two, one for me and one for yourself. We must not be captured alive." At this time, a bullet came and He Yun was hit and seriously injured. When medical staff arrived, he struggled and said: "My injury is not serious. Go and rescue the comrade who fell over there." This was his last words.

　　After He Yun's death, General Liu Bocheng lamented: "What a pity! One military general (Zuo Quan) and one literary general (He Yun), two great generals, sacrificed their lives for the country." Comrade Yang Shangkun wrote in "Mourning He Yun", "He Yun was in his prime, and the tasks given by the Party had yet to be completed with all his strength. Unfortunately, he died today. This is indeed a loss!"

　　In May this year, the Matan Eighth Route Army Headquarters Memorial Hall welcomed a steady stream of study groups. Every day, hundreds of students from Shanxi, Hebei, and Henan came to pay tribute to the Taihang Journalism Martyrs Monument and visit the Journalism Martyrs Exhibition Hall. Guide Yang Zhenyu said: "Every time after telling He Yun's story, the exhibition hall would always fall into a solemn silence, and many people could not help crying."

　　Yang Zhenyu still remembers the sharing of a visitor: "It was the firm belief in the victory of the war of resistance that made him bravely face death in desperation." What He Yun and his comrades interpreted with their lives was far more than the professional perseverance of the Party's journalists, but also the patriotism of the Chinese people at the time of national crisis. This has left an indelible spiritual mark in the magnificent picture of the Chinese nation's war of resistance.
translation_improvement_points:
1. The title's phrase "最后一颗子弹留给自己" is translated as "Save the last bullet for yourself." This version sounds like a command, whereas the original is a statement of intent. A more fitting translation would be "Leaving the last bullet for oneself," which better reflects the declarative and fateful tone.
2. The opening description "巍巍太行，苍山如刃" is translated as "The towering Taihang Mountains are like blades." This loses the poetic, parallel structure and the specific color of "苍" (cāng), which means grey, green, or azure. A more evocative translation would be "Majestic Taihang, its grey-green peaks like blades."
3. The historical term "大扫荡" (dà sǎodàng) is translated as "sweep." This is a significant understatement of the brutal "Three Alls Policy" (Kill All, Burn All, Loot All) it refers to. A more historically accurate term would be "brutal mopping-up campaign" or "scorched-earth offensive."
4. The phrase "摧残了他的身躯" (cuīcánle tā de shēnqū) is translated as "destroyed his body." The word "destroyed" is too absolute, given he could still function. A more nuanced translation like "ravaged his body" or "took a devastating toll on his body" would be more accurate.
5. The term "发刊词" (fākāncí) is translated as "introductory remarks." This is too generic. A more precise term would be "inaugural mission statement" or "founding editorial," clarifying it was for the newspaper's very first issue.
6. The phrase "与敌苦斗到底" (yǔ dí kǔ dòu dàodǐ) is translated as "fight the enemy to the end." This translation misses the nuance of "苦" (kǔ), which implies bitterness and hardship. A more faithful rendering is "to wage an arduous struggle against the enemy to the very end."
7. The description "手不停地挥动着毛笔" (shǒu bù tíng de huīdòngzhe máobǐ) is translated as "waving his brush non-stop." The word "waving" is an awkward verb for the act of writing. A more natural and dynamic phrasing would be "his brush moved unceasingly" or "wielding his brush without pause."
8. The term "主持战地新闻采访工作" (zhǔchí zhàndì xīnwén cǎifǎng gōngzuò) is translated as "preside over battlefield news interviews." This is slightly inaccurate, as "采访工作" (cǎifǎng gōngzuò) is broader than just "interviews." A better translation is "to direct battlefield reporting" or "to lead news-gathering efforts on the front lines."
9. The parallel structure "即审即刻、即校即印" (jí shěn jí kè, jí xiào jí yìn) is translated as "it was immediately reviewed, proofread and printed." The English version loses the rhythmic, staccato urgency of the original. A translation that better captures this style and includes the omitted step of "刻" (kè - engraving) would be: "it was instantly reviewed and engraved, proofread and printed."
10. The phrase "被重重包围" (bèi chóngchóng bāowéi) is translated simply as "surrounded." This misses the emphasis of "重重" (chóngchóng), which implies multiple layers or great intensity. A more accurate translation would be "tightly encircled" or "completely surrounded on all sides."
11. The phrase "抄收...电讯" (chāoshōu...diànxùn) is rendered as "copied the...telegrams." The term "抄收" is specific to transcribing radio transmissions. A more precise translation would be "transcribed...radio dispatches."
12. The culturally significant term "家国大义" (jiāguó dàyì) is simplified to "patriotism." This misses the deep-seated concept of a intertwined duty to both one's family (家) and nation (国). A more nuanced translation would be "the profound duty to one's home and country" or "the greater righteous cause of the nation."
13. The metaphor "壮阔画卷" (zhuàngkuò huàjuàn), meaning a grand, rolling scroll painting, is translated as "magnificent picture." "Picture" is rather plain and loses the scale of the original metaphor. "Magnificent tapestry" or "grand panorama" would be more evocative and faithful to the imagery.
14. The modern term "研学团队" (yánxué tuánduì) is translated as "study groups." This is too generic for a term that specifically refers to organized educational trips. "Educational tour groups" or "student research delegations" would be more accurate.
15. The phrase "对抗战必胜的坚定信念" (duì Kàngzhàn bì shèng de jiāndìng xìnniàn) is translated as "the firm belief in the victory of the war of resistance." The original, particularly "必胜" (bì shèng), implies a conviction in an inevitable outcome. A stronger translation would be "the firm conviction that victory in the War of Resistance was inevitable."

original_text:
题：中外学者期待中国减贫经验惠及更多国家

　　新华社记者施钱贵、谢佼

　　7月11日在全球文明对话部长级会议上，新华社国家高端智库面向全球发布中英文智库报告《为了人民美好生活接续奋斗——巩固拓展脱贫攻坚成果的中国实践与理论创新》，引发众多关注文明交流互鉴与全球发展繁荣的全球学者、官员热议。他们赞同报告，认为巩固拓展脱贫攻坚成果的中国实践为世界减贫作出了新示范，期待中国减贫经验惠及更多国家。

　　“我认真看了报告，对‘提高贫困地区人们的教育水平、让人们有能力实现自我发展’等内容印象深刻，非常受启发。”所罗门群岛教育与人力资源发展部部长托泽·里奥卡纳向记者表示，所罗门群岛教育体系还处于发展起步阶段，报告里有很多珍贵的内容值得学习。

　　马里共和国手工业、文化、旅馆业和旅游部长马穆·达飞说：“报告非常有价值，特别是展示了如何通过发展产业来让人民富裕。马里旅游资源丰富，中国减贫经验很有借鉴意义。”

　　“报告有宏观总结提炼，也有具体细化分析和实例支撑，是一部中国减贫学行动指南、实操手册。”中央党史和文献研究院科研规划部副主任李琦认为，该报告针对发展中国家需求，对中国巩固拓展脱贫攻坚成果的成就和经验进行总结和分析，对当今全球南方国家脱离贫困、实现发展繁荣具有非常强的直接借鉴和启发参考价值。

　　“比如设立5年过渡期，就是中国特色减贫道路的又一重大创举。”四川行政学院副院长胡雯教授表示，这不仅有利于持续帮扶守住脱贫成果，更为建立常态化长效化减贫机制、激发脱贫群众内生动力与能力、循序渐进接续乡村全面振兴发展，留足了“回旋”空间，是以人民为中心的发展观和实事求是的集中体现。

　　报告指出，巩固拓展脱贫攻坚成果同乡村振兴有效衔接5年过渡期的实践证明，“机制防返贫、基建补短板、就业稳增收、产业激活力、保障兜底线”的巩固脱贫模式，经受住了历史和人民的检验。这也向世界充分说明，贫困不仅是可以战胜的，在建立起可靠有效的后续帮扶机制的前提下，也是可以阻断、不再复发的。

　　“看到智库报告的发布，让扎根乡村的年轻人发展信心更足。”四川省德阳市中江县金穗源粮食专业合作社负责人常滔说，5年过渡期里，国家注重农业产业稳健发展，今后自己将进一步发挥带动作用，通过利益联结机制促进老百姓共同致富。

　　报告强调，作为发展中国家的重要一员，中国始终秉持构建人类命运共同体理念，愿以自身的减贫经验与积极行动，助力改变全球贫困现状。通过为各国减贫事业和现代化进程贡献智慧与方案，中国正与世界各国一道，努力共创一个没有贫困的美好世界。

　　“中国不仅解决了自己的贫困问题，更为全世界脱贫作出了贡献，树立了榜样。”知名经济学者、工信部信息通信经济专家委员会委员盘和林说，全球任何一个贫困国家，都可以通过研究中国减贫和防止返贫的经验，来为自身脱贫汲取养分。

　　国际关系学院教授、战略传播研究中心主任纪忠慧认为，中国特色脱贫攻坚理论，创新丰富发展了经济学理论，证明了经济增长与公平分配相互协同的可能性，为发展中国家探索了一条可操作的减贫路。

　　“这些宝贵的经验，值得‘全球南方’国家共同学习。”北京语言大学外籍专家、厄瓜多尔前交通部长孙海诚说，希望中国多分享减贫治理经验，展示如何实现乡村振兴，如何通过现代化的发展来有效减少贫困。
english_translation:
Xinhua News Agency, Beijing, July 12 : Chinese and foreign scholars expect China's poverty reduction experience to benefit more countries

　　Xinhua News Agency reporters Shi Qiangui and Xie Jiao

　　On July 11, at the Ministerial Conference on Global Civilization Dialogue, Xinhua News Agency's National High-end Think Tank released a Chinese and English think tank report titled "Continuing to Strive for a Better Life for the People - China's Practice and Theoretical Innovation in Consolidating and Expanding the Achievements of Poverty Alleviation" to the world, which triggered heated discussions among many global scholars and officials who are concerned about the exchange and mutual learning of civilizations and global development and prosperity. They agreed with the report, believing that China's practice of consolidating and expanding the achievements of poverty alleviation has set a new example for global poverty reduction, and they look forward to China's poverty reduction experience benefiting more countries.

　　"I read the report carefully and was impressed by the content such as 'improving the education level of people in poor areas and enabling them to achieve self-development'. I was very inspired." Tozer Riocana, Minister of Education and Human Resources Development of the Solomon Islands, told reporters that the education system of the Solomon Islands is still in its early stages of development and there are many valuable contents in the report that are worth learning.

　　Mamoud Dafy, Minister of Handicrafts, Culture, Hotel Industry and Tourism of the Republic of Mali, said: "The report is very valuable, especially in showing how to make people rich by developing industries. Mali is rich in tourism resources, and China's poverty alleviation experience is very instructive."

　　"The report has a macro summary and distillation, as well as specific detailed analysis and examples. It is an action guide and practical manual for China's poverty reduction studies." Li Qi, deputy director of the Scientific Research and Planning Department of the Central Party History and Literature Research Institute, believes that the report summarizes and analyzes China's achievements and experience in consolidating and expanding the results of poverty alleviation in response to the needs of developing countries. It has a very strong direct reference and inspirational reference value for today's global South countries to escape poverty and achieve development and prosperity.

　　"For example, the establishment of a five-year transition period is another major innovation in China's poverty reduction path." Professor Hu Wen, vice president of Sichuan Administrative College, said that this will not only help to continuously help maintain the results of poverty alleviation, but also establish a normalized and long-term poverty reduction mechanism, stimulate the endogenous motivation and ability of people out of poverty, and gradually continue the comprehensive revitalization and development of rural areas, leaving enough "room for maneuvering". It is a concentrated embodiment of the people-centered development concept and the pragmatic approach.

　　The report pointed out that the practice of the five-year transition period of consolidating and expanding the achievements of poverty alleviation and effectively linking rural revitalization has proved that the poverty alleviation model of "mechanism to prevent poverty relapse, infrastructure to make up for shortcomings, employment to stabilize income, industry to stimulate vitality, and bottom line guarantee" has withstood the test of history and the people. This also fully demonstrates to the world that poverty can not only be overcome, but also blocked and will not recur under the premise of establishing a reliable and effective follow-up assistance mechanism.

　　"Seeing the release of the think tank report gives young people who have settled in rural areas more confidence in their development." Chang Tao, head of the Jinsuiyuan Grain Professional Cooperative in Zhongjiang County, Deyang City, Sichuan Province, said that during the five-year transition period, the country focused on the steady development of the agricultural industry. In the future, he will further play a leading role and promote the common prosperity of the people through an interest linkage mechanism.

　　The report emphasizes that as an important member of the developing countries, China has always adhered to the concept of building a community with a shared future for mankind and is willing to help change the global poverty situation with its own poverty reduction experience and positive actions. By contributing wisdom and solutions to the poverty reduction and modernization process of various countries, China is working with other countries in the world to create a better world without poverty.

　　"China has not only solved its own poverty problem, but also contributed to poverty alleviation around the world and set an example." Pan Helin, a well-known economist and member of the Information and Communications Economics Expert Committee of the Ministry of Industry and Information Technology, said that any poor country in the world can draw nourishment for its own poverty alleviation by studying China's experience in poverty reduction and preventing relapse into poverty.

　　Ji Zhonghui, professor at the School of International Relations and director of the Strategic Communication Research Center, believes that China's theory of poverty alleviation has innovatively enriched and developed economic theory, proved the possibility of mutual coordination between economic growth and fair distribution, and explored a feasible path to poverty reduction for developing countries.

　　"These valuable experiences are worthy of learning from by countries in the 'global South'." Sun Haicheng, a foreign expert at Beijing Language and Culture University and former Minister of Transport of Ecuador, said that he hopes China will share more of its poverty reduction governance experience, demonstrate how to achieve rural revitalization, and how to effectively reduce poverty through modern development.
translation_improvement_points:
1. The term "脱贫攻坚" (tuōpín gōngjiān) is translated as "poverty alleviation." This translation misses the intensity of "攻坚" (gōngjiān), which literally means "to storm a fortified position." A more faithful translation would be "the fight against poverty" or "the critical battle against poverty" to better convey the original's sense of a difficult, determined national campaign.
2. The phrase "借鉴意义" (jièjiàn yìyì) is translated as "very instructive." The term "借鉴" (jièjiàn) specifically implies learning from a model or example for one's own use. A more precise rendering would be "offers valuable lessons" or "provides a useful model for reference."
3. The phrase "让人民富裕" (ràng rénmín fùyù) is translated as "to make people rich." This is overly blunt and simplistic. A translation like "to bring prosperity to the people" or "to enrich the populace" would better match the formal tone of the article and the broader concept of wealth and well-being implied by "富裕" (fùyù).
4. The key political term "实事求是" (shíshì qiú shì) is translated as "pragmatic approach." This captures only part of the meaning. The phrase has deep philosophical and political roots in the CPC context, meaning "seeking truth from facts." A more accurate translation would be "the principle of seeking truth from facts" or a "fact-based approach" to preserve this important nuance.
5. The description of the poverty alleviation model contains the phrase "保障兜底线" (bǎozhàng dōu dǐxiàn), translated as "bottom line guarantee." This is a literalism that is opaque in English. The phrase refers to providing a basic safety net, so a clearer, more functional translation is "providing a social safety net" or "ensuring basic living standards."
6. The term "扎根乡村" (zhāgēn xiāngcūn) is translated as "settled in rural areas." The verb "扎根" (zhāgēn) means "to take root," which implies a far deeper and more permanent commitment than simply "to settle." A better translation is "young people who are rooted in the countryside" or "who have put down roots in rural areas."
7. The technical term "利益联结机制" (lìyì liánjié jīzhì) is translated literally as "interest linkage mechanism." This is unclear jargon in English. A more explanatory translation like "benefit-sharing mechanisms" or "mechanisms for aligning economic interests" would be much clearer to a non-specialist reader.
8. The metaphor "汲取养分" (jíqǔ yǎngfèn) is translated literally as "draw nourishment." While understandable, it sounds poetic and unnatural in a formal news context. A more idiomatic English equivalent would be "to draw valuable lessons" or "to gain crucial insights."
9. The term "相互协同" (xiānghù xiétóng), used to describe the relationship between economic growth and fair distribution, is translated as "mutual coordination." A more precise and common term in economic discourse for this concept is "synergy" or a "synergistic relationship."
10. The official term "中国特色减贫道路" (Zhōngguó tèsè jiǎnpín dàolù) is shortened to "China's poverty reduction path." This omits the key political branding of "特色" (tèsè). The standard and more accurate translation is "the path of poverty reduction with Chinese characteristics."
11. The phrase "接续奋斗" (jiēxù fèndòu) in the report's title is translated as "Continuing to Strive." The original implies a relentless, ongoing struggle passed from one stage to the next. "Sustained Endeavor" or "An Ongoing Struggle" would better capture this nuance of determined continuity and arduous effort.
12. In the list of poverty alleviation model components, "基建补短板" (jījiàn bǔ duǎnbǎn) is translated as "infrastructure to make up for shortcomings." The idiom "补短板" (bǔ duǎnbǎn) means to shore up weaknesses. A more idiomatic and professional-sounding translation would be "addressing infrastructure gaps" or "shoring up infrastructure deficiencies."

original_text:
时下，智能手机已成为人们获取信息、社交娱乐的重要工具。多学科专家指出，当我们刷手机出现注意力极度涣散、缺乏独立思考能力、情绪化反应严重、深度学习能力下降、空虚感增加等症状时，就表明我们的大脑正在被“腐蚀”。所谓的“脑腐”，即因长期暴露于碎片化信息而引发的认知衰退与脑功能损伤。

  对多数人来说，手机早已成为生活的一部分，每天睡醒第一件事就是打开手机，睡前最后一件事就是关掉手机。频繁地刷手机并沉迷其中，的确存在诸多危害，如影响注意力、思考力、学习力等。专家们将这一现象形象地描述为“脑腐”，这是一种定义，也是一种预警。当人类的认知能力、情绪管理、社交技能等在无形中被“腐蚀”时，人类引以为傲的理性思维，正在面临前所未有的困境与挑战。

  “脑腐”现象的出现，存在极大的负面影响。一方面，“脑腐”会给人们的身心健康带来威胁，影响人的激素分泌，破坏大脑的自我修复，对青少年的健康影响更甚。另一方面，“脑腐”还会带来恶性循环。注意力碎片化会导致认知能力下滑，认知能力下滑又让人难以抵挡诱惑，进而加剧对手机的依赖。这种循环最可怕的地方在于，它悄无声息地改变了大脑的工作方式，使人们失去专注阅读、逻辑推理和独立思考的能力，而这些，恰恰是文明社会赖以维系的核心技能。

  面对这一现象，简单的技术戒断，显然不是解决问题的方案。我们需要建立的是多层次立体化的“防御体系”。比如，在家庭层面，可以制定一个“戒断”时间表，通过家庭成员的互帮互助努力克服这一难题；在学校层面，积极重建阅读文化，并通过深度阅读、娱乐活动、体育锻炼等替代选择，逐步告别手机成瘾；在社会层面，要努力完善网络成瘾的干预机制，如推出睡眠保护计划、进行替代性认知训练等，凝聚起全社会更大的共识。

  技术进步不可阻挡，我们需要做的是重新确立人的主体地位。法国著名哲学家贝尔纳·斯蒂格勒曾经说过：“技术是人类的解药，也是人类的毒药。我们要对技术的高速发展心存警惕。”当智能手机开始“腐蚀”人类最珍贵的心智能力时，我们就必须重新设置使用边界、培养批判意识、重建深度社交。我们需要创造一个新的技术文化，去应对技术的时代，唯有如此，我们才能在享受技术便利的同时，不至于迷失在数字迷雾中，丧失人之为人的思维与尊严。

  在“脑腐”现象的认知保卫战中，每个人都是参与者。我们需要放下手机、重拾书本，这不仅是在修复自己的大脑，也是给人类文明的未来投票。如此，我们才能更从容地应对日益复杂的各种风险与挑战。 （龙敏飞）
english_translation:
Nowadays, smartphones have become an important tool for people to obtain information, socialize and entertain. Multidisciplinary experts point out that when we use our phones and experience symptoms such as extreme lack of concentration, lack of independent thinking ability, severe emotional reactions, decreased deep learning ability, and increased sense of emptiness, it means that our brains are being "corroded." The so-called "brain rot" refers to cognitive decline and brain function damage caused by long-term exposure to fragmented information.

  For most people, mobile phones have long become a part of life. The first thing they do when they wake up every day is to turn on their phones, and the last thing they do before going to bed is to turn off their phones. Frequent use of mobile phones and addiction to them do have many harmful effects, such as affecting attention, thinking, and learning abilities. Experts have vividly described this phenomenon as "brain rot," which is both a definition and a warning. When human cognitive abilities, emotional management, and social skills are invisibly "corroded," the rational thinking that humans are proud of is facing unprecedented difficulties and challenges.

  The emergence of the "brain rot" phenomenon has a huge negative impact. On the one hand, "brain rot" will threaten people's physical and mental health, affect people's hormone secretion, destroy the brain's self-repair, and have a greater impact on the health of teenagers. On the other hand, "brain rot" will also bring about a vicious cycle. Fragmented attention will lead to a decline in cognitive ability, and the decline in cognitive ability will make it difficult for people to resist temptation, which will in turn increase their dependence on mobile phones. The most terrifying thing about this cycle is that it quietly changes the way the brain works, causing people to lose the ability to concentrate on reading, logical reasoning, and independent thinking, which are precisely the core skills that civilized society relies on.

  In the face of this phenomenon, simply quitting using technology is obviously not a solution to the problem. What we need to establish is a multi-level, three-dimensional "defense system." For example, at the family level, a "quit" schedule can be drawn up to help family members overcome this problem through mutual help; at the school level, we should actively rebuild the reading culture and gradually say goodbye to mobile phone addiction through alternative choices such as in-depth reading, entertainment activities, and physical exercise; at the social level, we should strive to improve the intervention mechanism for Internet addiction, such as launching a sleep protection plan, conducting alternative cognitive training, etc., to build a greater consensus across society.

  Technological progress is unstoppable. What we need to do is to re-establish the subjectivity of human beings. The famous French philosopher Bernard Stiegler once said: "Technology is both the antidote and poison of mankind. We must be vigilant against the rapid development of technology." When smartphones begin to "corrode" the most precious mental abilities of human beings, we must reset the boundaries of use, cultivate critical awareness, and rebuild deep social interaction. We need to create a new technological culture to cope with the era of technology. Only in this way can we enjoy the convenience of technology without getting lost in the digital fog and losing our human thinking and dignity.

  In the battle to defend the cognitive function of "brain rot", everyone is a participant. We need to put down our phones and pick up books again. This is not only to repair our brains, but also to vote for the future of human civilization. In this way, we can more calmly deal with the increasingly complex risks and challenges. (Long Minfei)
translation_improvement_points:
1. The term "刷手机" (shuā shǒujī), translated as "use our phones," is more accurately rendered as "scrolling on our phones." This better captures the specific, passive, and continuous action of swiping through content that the article critiques.
2. The phrase "注意力极度涣散" (zhùyìlì jídù huànsàn) is translated as "extreme lack of concentration." A more nuanced translation like "extreme distractibility" or "severely scattered attention" would better convey the original's meaning of attention being dispersed rather than just absent.
3. The phrase "人类引以为傲的理性思维" (rénlèi yǐn yǐ wéi ào de lǐxìng sīwéi), translated as "the rational thinking that humans are proud of," could be improved for tone and naturalness to "humanity's proudest faculty: rational thought" or "the capacity for rational thought, a hallmark of humanity."
4. The clause "对青少年的健康影响更甚" (duì qīngshàonián de jiànkāng yǐngxiǎng gèng shèn) is translated as "have a greater impact on the health of teenagers." The term "更甚" (gèng shèn) implies a more severe degree, which would be better captured by "with an even more severe impact on the health of adolescents."
5. The term "赖以维系" (lài yǐ wéixì), translated as "relies on," understates the original's gravity. A more impactful translation would be "that form the bedrock of civilized society" or "on which civilized society depends for its very foundation."
6. The term "技术戒断" (jìshù jièduàn) is translated as "quitting using technology," which is too absolute. A more precise term would be "digital detox" or "technological withdrawal," referring to a temporary and targeted cessation of addictive use, not all technology.
7. The phrase "多层次立体化的'防御体系'" (duō céngcì lìtǐhuà de 'fángyù tǐxì') is translated literally as "a multi-level, three-dimensional 'defense system'." The term "立体化" (lìtǐhuà) here implies a comprehensive, all-encompassing nature, better translated as "a multi-layered, holistic 'defense system'."
8. The expression "逐步告别手机成瘾" (zhúbù gàobié shǒujī chéngyǐn), translated as "gradually say goodbye to mobile phone addiction," is slightly quaint. A more standard and direct phrasing would be "gradually overcome mobile phone addiction" or "progressively wean themselves off phone addiction."
9. The phrase "重新确立人的主体地位" (chóngxīn quèlì rén de zhǔtǐ dìwèi), translated as "re-establish the subjectivity of human beings," uses a philosophical term that might be unclear. A more accessible and potent translation is "reassert human agency" or "re-establish humanity's central role" over technology.
10. The term "深度社交" (shēndù shèjiāo) in "重建深度社交" is translated as "deep social interaction." This could be made more natural-sounding in English as "rebuild meaningful social connections" or "re-establish genuine social interaction."
11. The phrase "丧失人之为人的思维与尊严" (sàngshī rén zhī wéi rén de sīwéi yǔ zūnyán) is translated as "losing our human thinking and dignity." The nuance of "人之为人" (rén zhī wéi rén), meaning "that which makes us human," is lost. A fuller translation would be "losing the thinking and dignity that define our very humanity."
12. The phrase "在“脑腐”现象的认知保卫战中" (zài 'nǎo fǔ' xiànxiàng de rènzhī bǎowèi zhàn zhōng) is translated illogically as "In the battle to defend the cognitive function of 'brain rot'." A more accurate and logical rendering would be "In the cognitive battle against the 'brain rot' phenomenon" or "In the fight to defend our cognition from 'brain rot.'"
13. The adverb "从容地" (cóngróng de), translated as "calmly," implies more than just calmness; it suggests poise, composure, and being unhurried. A better translation would be "with greater poise" or "more capably and with composure."

original_text:
据中央网信办通报，2025年6月，网络谣言主要聚焦社会热点、灾情事故、伪科普等领域，造谣者通过移花接木、AI生成、伪科学包装等手段虚构或夸大事实，扰乱正常社会秩序。

  通报特别提到了灾情事故类谣言中，AI制假信息所产生的破坏力。有网民借机编造“湖南永州蓝山县特大暴雨致汽车被冲走”“福建南平建瓯房道镇路面严重积水”“乌鲁木齐南山板房沟突发山洪”等虚假视频和图文。这类谣言不仅加剧社会恐慌情绪，还干扰正常防灾救灾秩序，可谓贻害无穷。

  AI生成广泛存在于信息传播、内容创作等多个领域，在给工作生活带来便利、丰富创作手段、拓展阅读空间的同时，也给信息传播造成了很大的困扰。伪造名人演讲的深度合成视频、以假乱真的“伪科普”文章，还有各种事发现场的照片视频，AI制假信息凭借其高仿真度，正在消解公众的信息甄别能力。

  可以说，信息的造假问题已经成为互联网的一颗毒瘤，不治理将侵蚀网络社会的信任基础。无论是从哪方面看，都要求我们必须对AI内容进行严格区分，让AI合成内容回归它本来的面目。

  面对AI合成造假的新问题，需要升级管理办法。目前比较常用的防治办法是进行标识，让AI合成内容公之于众，晒于阳光之下。当下，AI制假信息之所以能兴风作浪，很大程度上就是因为它伪装得好，别人看不穿看不透，在真假难辨间钻空子。AI合成内容标识清楚了，这个空间自然也就消失了。

  与此相关的法律法规正在完善。今年9月1日起施行制定的《人工智能生成合成内容标识办法》进一步对如何标识作出了详细规定，届时，相关法律法规将一起形成组合拳，堵住AI合成内容治理漏洞。

  同时，平台应该进一步督促、约束作者严格执行相关规定，建立起一套标识上可溯源、技术上可反制、法律责任界定上清晰明确的监管机制，让平台算法的抓取和推荐成为网络空间善治的模范，而不是谣言传播的帮凶。此外，AI模型公司也该积极参与治理，模型公司有必要在技术层面嵌入不可篡改的标识模块。

  历史经验表明，每次技术革命都伴随着治理模式的迭代。从印刷品时代的知识产权保护，到互联网时代的网络空间治理，人类始终在寻找创新与秩序的平衡点。互联网社会的基础是诚信，只有守住真实传播的底线，互联网才能在技术创新中保持旺盛的生命力。 （高路）
english_translation:
According to a report from the Cyberspace Administration of China, in June 2025, online rumors mainly focused on social hot spots, disasters and accidents, pseudo-science and other fields. Rumor mongers fabricated or exaggerated facts through means such as grafting, AI generation, and pseudo-scientific packaging, disrupting normal social order.

  The report specifically mentioned the destructive power of AI-generated fake information in rumors about disasters and accidents. Some netizens took the opportunity to fabricate false videos and pictures such as "Heavy rain in Lanshan County, Yongzhou, Hunan caused cars to be washed away", "Severe waterlogging on the road in Fangdao Town, Jianou, Nanping, Fujian", and "Sudden flash floods in Banfanggou, Nanshan, Urumqi". Such rumors not only exacerbate social panic, but also interfere with the normal order of disaster prevention and relief, which can be said to cause endless harm.

  AI generation is widely used in many fields such as information dissemination and content creation. While it brings convenience to work and life, enriches creative means, and expands reading space, it also causes great trouble to information dissemination. Deep synthetic videos of fake celebrity speeches, fake and realistic "pseudo-popular science" articles, and photos and videos of various incidents, AI-generated fake information is eroding the public's ability to discern information with its high degree of simulation.

  It can be said that the problem of information fraud has become a cancer on the Internet. If it is not managed, it will erode the trust foundation of the online society. No matter from which perspective, we must strictly distinguish AI content and return AI-synthesized content to its original appearance.

  In the face of new problems of AI synthetic fraud, it is necessary to upgrade the management methods. At present, the more commonly used prevention and control method is to mark it and make AI synthetic content public and exposed to the sun. At present, the reason why AI fake information can stir up trouble is largely because it is well disguised and others cannot see through it, and it takes advantage of the gap between the true and the false. If AI synthetic content is clearly marked, this space will naturally disappear.

  The relevant laws and regulations are being improved. The "Measures for Identifying Synthetic Content Generated by Artificial Intelligence" which came into effect on September 1 this year further made detailed provisions on how to identify it. At that time, the relevant laws and regulations will form a combination of punches to plug the loopholes in the governance of AI synthetic content.

  At the same time, the platform should further urge and constrain authors to strictly implement relevant regulations, establish a set of regulatory mechanisms that can be traced in terms of identification, countered in terms of technology, and clearly defined in terms of legal responsibilities, so that the platform algorithm's capture and recommendation can become a model of good governance in cyberspace, rather than an accomplice in the spread of rumors. In addition, AI model companies should also actively participate in governance, and it is necessary for model companies to embed unalterable identification modules at the technical level.

  Historical experience shows that every technological revolution is accompanied by the iteration of governance models. From intellectual property protection in the print era to cyberspace governance in the Internet era, humans have always been looking for a balance between innovation and order. The foundation of the Internet society is integrity. Only by keeping the bottom line of true communication can the Internet maintain its vitality in technological innovation. (Gao Lu)
translation_improvement_points:
1. The term "通报" (tōngbào) is translated as "report." A more precise term would be "bulletin" or "official notice" to better convey the authoritative and public-facing nature of the announcement from the Cyberspace Administration.
2. The idiom "移花接木" (yí huā jiē mù), translated as "grafting," is too literal and potentially obscure. A clearer, more descriptive phrase for this digital context would be "deceptive editing" or "splicing unrelated content together" to create a false narrative.
3. The strong idiom "可谓贻害无穷" (kěwèi yí hài wú qióng) is translated somewhat weakly as "which can be said to cause endless harm." A more impactful rendering that captures the original's severe tone would be "causing immeasurable harm" or "posing a limitless threat."
4. The common term for "深度合成视频" (shēndù héchéng shìpín) in English is "deepfake videos." The translation "Deep synthetic videos" is a literal rendering but less idiomatic and may not be immediately recognizable to the reader.
5. The phrase "以假乱真的'伪科普'文章" (yǐ jiǎ luàn zhēn de 'wěi kēxué' wénzhāng) is translated as "fake and realistic 'pseudo-popular science' articles." A better translation would be "hyper-realistic 'pseudo-science' articles" or "'pseudo-science' articles so realistic they appear genuine," which avoids the slightly contradictory "fake and realistic" and better captures the meaning of the idiom "以假乱真."
6. The term "高仿真度" (gāo fǎngzhēn dù), translated as "high degree of simulation," would sound more natural in English as "high fidelity" or "high degree of realism."
7. In the sentence "不治理将侵蚀网络社会的信任基础" (bù zhìlǐ jiāng qīnshí...), the word "治理" (zhìlǐ) is translated as "managed." A stronger term like "governed," "regulated," or the phrase "If left unchecked" would better reflect the original's sense of urgency and the need for active intervention.
8. The phrase "回归它本来的面目" (huíguī tā běnlái de miànmù) is translated as "return to its original appearance." This is too literal. A more figurative and accurate translation would be "to be seen for what it truly is" or "to reveal its true nature," which captures the idea of unmasking the AI content.
9. The idiom "兴风作浪" (xīng fēng zuò làng), translated as "stir up trouble," could be translated with more force as "wreak havoc" or "proliferate unchecked" to better convey the scale of disruption implied by the original Chinese.
10. The phrase "在真假难辨间钻空子" (zài zhēn jiǎ nán biàn jiān zuān kòngzi) is translated as "it takes advantage of the gap between the true and the false." A more idiomatic and concise translation would be "exploiting the difficulty in distinguishing fact from fiction" or "exploiting the ambiguity between truth and falsehood."
11. The translation states the "Measures for Identifying Synthetic Content..." "came into effect on September 1 this year." However, the original "今年9月1日起施行" (jīnnián jiǔ yuè yī rì qǐ shīxíng) indicates a future action: "will be implemented starting from September 1 of this year." The tense in the translation is incorrect and misrepresents the status of the law.
12. The term "组合拳" (zǔhé quán), a boxing metaphor, is translated literally as "a combination of punches." A more common and natural-sounding English equivalent would be "a concerted effort" or "a multi-pronged approach" to describe the combined effect of the laws.
13. The structure "标识上可溯源、技术上可反制、法律责任界定上清晰明确" loses its parallel structure in translation. A clearer rendering would be: "a regulatory mechanism with traceable identifiers, technical countermeasures, and clearly defined legal responsibilities."
14. The phrase "守住真实传播的底线" (shǒuzhù zhēnshí chuánbō de dǐxiàn) is translated literally as "keeping the bottom line of true communication." A more natural English phrasing would be "upholding the principle of truthful dissemination" or "maintaining a baseline of factual communication."
15. The term "旺盛的生命力" (wàngshèng de shēngmìnglì), translated as "its vitality," loses some of its intensity. "旺盛" implies vigor and exuberance. A more nuanced translation would be "its robust vitality" or "its vibrant life force."

original_text:
当游客在大理古城遭遇“一对一”导购死缠烂打，当西双版纳的导游因游客不购物便恶语相向，这些屡禁不止的旅游乱象，不仅撕碎了“七彩云南”的美好画卷，更透支着消费者对旅游市场的信任。近日云南省出台的旅游市场诚信建设10条措施，以“对事对人”的刚性约束剑指沉疴，为净化旅游生态注入了制度强心剂。

  此次新政最鲜明的特征，在于打破了“违法成本低、守法成本高”的怪圈。过去，一些旅行社将不合理低价游作为引流利器，即便被查处也只需缴纳罚款，换个马甲就能重操旧业；个别导游靠强制购物牟取暴利，吊销证件后仍能通过灰色渠道继续带团。这种“只罚机构、不追个人”的监管漏洞，使得一些旅游乱象陷入“整治-反弹-再整治”的恶性循环。而新规明确“旅行社吊销执照则负责人5年禁业、导游吊证则终身不得再考”，相当于给违法者戴上了“职业紧箍”，让失信者真正付出了代价、切实感受到肉疼。

  政策的智慧更体现在刚柔并济的治理哲学中。对诚信经营主体实施“有求必应，无事不扰”的监管原则，既减少了行政干预对市场活力的束缚，又通过“红榜”激励机制引导行业向善；对失信行为分级施策，则避免了“一刀切”式监管可能造成的误伤，让监管力量精准聚焦于扰乱市场秩序的“害群之马”。这种区分对待的治理逻辑，恰似为旅游市场安装了“信用过滤器”，既能过滤杂质，又能留住良币。

  治理旅游市场乱象，从来不是简单的行政处罚问题，而是关乎产业根基的系统工程。当低价游陷阱让游客望而却步，强制购物的阴影笼罩旅途，受损的不仅是消费者权益，更是地方旅游的金字招牌。云南此次以信用监管为核心的制度设计，本质上是通过重塑市场规则，让旅游产业回归服务为本的初心。只有当导游不必靠诱导购物谋生，旅行社无需靠虚假宣传竞争，旅游市场才能形成“服务质量决定生存空间”的良性循环，游客才能真正享受“诗和远方”的惬意。

  实际上，伴随着暑期旅游出行高峰来临，为了让大家更舒心、更安心出游，文旅部联合公安、市场监管等部门已经在全国范围内开展旅游市场秩序整治，重点整治强迫购物等违法违规行为。此外，多地出台有力举措，守护“火热”暑期游。例如，针对无证游商、占道扰序、黄牛倒票等涉旅乱象，公安机关和相关部门还积极开展联合执法，对出租房屋、仓库等地进行清查，对非法经营等情况进行源头治理。

  制度的生命力在于执行。如何确保禁业名单不流于形式？怎样防止“红榜”评选出现猫腻？这些现实问题考验着监管部门的执行力与智慧。或许可以借鉴“旅游黑名单”全国联网的经验，将各地的信用监管数据接入国家平台，让失信者在全国范围内寸步难行；同时引入第三方评估机构，让诚信激励机制更加公开透明。如此，才能让纸面规定进一步转化为市场治理的实效。

  从云南旅游市场的治理迭代，可以看出中国旅游产业转型升级的必然要求。此次10条措施的出台，不仅是对当下乱象的有力回应，更标志着旅游监管从“运动式整治”向“制度化治理”的关键跨越。每个市场主体都应清楚“失信必受罚、违法必严惩”，而当诚信经营成为行业共识，地方旅游名片必将焕发光彩，旅游市场也将在制度护航下走向更高质量的发展阶段。 （关育兵）
english_translation:
When tourists in Dali Ancient Town are pestered by "one-on-one" shopping guides, and when tour guides in Xishuangbanna verbally abuse tourists because they do not shop, these tourism chaos that have been repeatedly banned not only tear up the beautiful picture of "Colorful Yunnan", but also overdraw consumers' trust in the tourism market. Recently, Yunnan Province issued 10 measures to build integrity in the tourism market, which target chronic diseases with rigid constraints of "things and people", and inject institutional boosters into purifying the tourism ecology.

  The most distinctive feature of the new policy is that it breaks the vicious circle of "low cost of breaking the law and high cost of abiding by the law". In the past, some travel agencies used unreasonable low-price tours as a tool to attract customers. Even if they were investigated, they only had to pay a fine and change their vests to resume their old business; some tour guides made huge profits by forcing shopping, and they could continue to lead groups through gray channels after their certificates were revoked. This regulatory loophole of "only punishing institutions and not pursuing individuals" has caused some tourism chaos to fall into a vicious cycle of "rectification-rebound-re-rectification". The new regulations clearly state that "if a travel agency's license is revoked, the person in charge will be banned from the industry for 5 years, and if a tour guide's license is revoked, he will not be allowed to take the exam again for life", which is equivalent to putting a "professional hoop" on the offenders, making the dishonest people really pay the price and feel the pain.

  The wisdom of the policy is more reflected in the governance philosophy of combining firmness and flexibility. The implementation of the supervision principle of "responding to requests and not disturbing when there is no need" for honest business entities not only reduces the constraints of administrative intervention on market vitality, but also guides the industry to be good through the "red list" incentive mechanism; the classification of dishonest behaviors avoids the possible accidental injuries caused by "one-size-fits-all" supervision, and allows the supervision force to focus precisely on the "black sheep" that disrupt the market order. This governance logic of differentiated treatment is like installing a "credit filter" for the tourism market, which can filter out impurities and retain good money.

  Governing the chaos in the tourism market has never been a simple administrative penalty issue, but a systematic project related to the foundation of the industry. When low-price tour traps discourage tourists and the shadow of forced shopping looms over the journey, it is not only the rights of consumers that are damaged, but also the golden signboard of local tourism. Yunnan's institutional design with credit supervision as the core is essentially to return the tourism industry to its original intention of service-oriented by reshaping market rules. Only when tour guides do not have to make a living by inducing shopping and travel agencies do not have to compete by false propaganda, can the tourism market form a virtuous circle of "service quality determines living space" and tourists can truly enjoy the comfort of "poetry and distant places".

  In fact, with the arrival of the peak of summer tourism, in order to make everyone feel more comfortable and at ease when traveling, the Ministry of Culture and Tourism, together with the public security, market supervision and other departments, has carried out tourism market order rectification across the country, focusing on rectifying illegal and irregular behaviors such as forced shopping. In addition, many places have introduced powerful measures to protect the "hot" summer tourism. For example, in response to unlicensed peddlers, road occupation, scalpers and other tourism-related chaos, the public security organs and relevant departments have also actively carried out joint law enforcement, conducted inspections of rental houses, warehouses and other places, and dealt with illegal operations at the source.

  The vitality of the system lies in its implementation. How to ensure that the list of banned industries does not become a formality? How to prevent the "red list" from being shady? These practical problems test the execution and wisdom of the regulatory authorities. Perhaps we can learn from the experience of the national networking of the "tourism blacklist" and connect the credit supervision data of various places to the national platform, making it difficult for dishonest people to move around the country; at the same time, introduce third-party evaluation agencies to make the credit incentive mechanism more open and transparent. Only in this way can the paper regulations be further transformed into the actual results of market governance.

  From the governance iteration of Yunnan's tourism market, we can see the inevitable requirement for the transformation and upgrading of China's tourism industry. The introduction of the 10 measures is not only a powerful response to the current chaos, but also marks a key leap from "campaign-style rectification" to "institutionalized governance" in tourism supervision. Every market player should be clear that "dishonesty must be punished and violations must be severely punished." When honest management becomes an industry consensus, local tourism business cards will surely shine, and the tourism market will also move towards a higher-quality development stage under the protection of the system. (Guan Yubing)
translation_improvement_points:
1. The idiom "死缠烂打" (sǐchánlàndǎ), translated as "pestered by," has a much stronger connotation of relentless, aggressive harassment. A more faithful translation would be "relentlessly harassed" or "hounded by" to capture the severe nature of the tourist experience.
2. The phrase "美好画卷" (měihǎo huàjuàn) is translated as "beautiful picture." The term "画卷" (huàjuàn) specifically refers to a 'picture scroll,' evoking a grand, panoramic image. A translation like "beautiful scroll" or "magnificent tapestry" would better preserve this imagery.
3. The term "沉疴" (chénkē), translated as "chronic diseases," is a literary metaphor for deep-seated, persistent problems. "Chronic ills" or "deep-rooted afflictions" would better capture the figurative and serious tone of the original.
4. The metaphor "制度强心剂" (zhìdù qiángxīnjì), translated as "institutional boosters," literally means an 'institutional cardiac stimulant' or 'shot in the arm.' A more dynamic translation like "a powerful institutional remedy" or "an institutional shot in the arm" would be more evocative.
5. The phrase "换个马甲" (huàn gè mǎjiǎ), translated as "change their vests," is a modern slang term for creating a new identity to evade consequences. This is lost in the literal translation. A more idiomatic rendering would be "rebrand under a new name" or "operate under a new guise."
6. The term "职业紧箍" (zhíyè jǐngū), translated as "professional hoop," fails to convey the powerful cultural reference to the inescapable, constricting headband from the classic novel *Journey to the West*. A better translation would be "professional straitjacket" or "career-ending restraint" to communicate the severity of the penalty.
7. The colloquial phrase "切实感受到肉疼" (qièshí gǎnshòu dào ròuténg), translated as "feel the pain," refers to a visceral, personal sting, often financial. "Truly feel the pinch" or "feel a tangible sting" would more accurately capture this specific, colloquial meaning.
8. The policy principle "有求必应，无事不扰" (yǒu qiú bì yìng, wú shì bù rǎo) is translated literally and a bit clumsily. A more polished and natural phrasing would be "responsive when needed, non-intrusive otherwise" or "always available to help, but otherwise hands-off."
9. The phrase "留住良币" (liú zhù liángbì), translated as "retain good money," is an allusion to Gresham's law ("good money drives out bad"). In this context, "良币" (liángbì) refers to reputable businesses, not currency. A clearer translation would be "retain the good actors" or "protect reputable businesses."
10. The term "金字招牌" (jīnzì zhāopái), translated as "golden signboard," is an idiom for a sterling reputation. "Gold-standard reputation" or "cherished brand" would be a more natural and idiomatic English equivalent.
11. The phrase "回归服务为本的初心" (huíguī fúwù wéi běn de chūxīn) is translated in a wordy manner. "初心" (chūxīn) is a key term meaning 'original aspiration.' A more concise translation is "return to the original mission of being service-oriented."
12. The popular phrase "诗和远方" (shī hé yuǎnfāng), translated as "poetry and distant places," loses some of its cultural weight. It represents a romanticized ideal of travel and escape from the mundane. While the literal translation is acceptable, it's worth noting it refers to the "romantic allure of travel" or "the idyllic escape."
13. The adjective "火热" (huǒrè) in "‘火热’暑期游," translated as "'hot' summer tourism," implies booming activity and high demand, not just temperature. "Booming" or "red-hot" summer tourism would be more precise.
14. The idiom "寸步难行" (cùn bù nán xíng), translated as "difficult... to move around," is significantly weakened. The original means 'unable to move even an inch' or 'completely immobilized.' A better rendering would be "rendering offenders unable to operate anywhere in the country."
15. The term "治理迭代" (zhìlǐ diédài), translated as "governance iteration," uses technical jargon. A more accessible translation would be "the evolution of governance" or "the next phase in governance," which preserves the meaning of progressive change without sounding overly technical.
16. The metaphor "地方旅游名片" (dìfāng lǚyóu míngpiàn), translated as "local tourism business cards," is an awkward literalism. "名片" here means a 'calling card' or 'hallmark.' A superior translation would be "the region's tourism brand" or "local tourism's calling card."
17. The verb "护航" (hùháng) in "制度护航" is translated as "protection." The original means 'to escort' or 'to convoy,' implying active guidance and safeguarding. A more accurate translation would be "under the aegis of the system" or "safeguarded by the new institutional framework."

original_text:
新华社北京7月11日电（记者刘硕）记者11日从最高人民检察院获悉，2025年上半年全国检察机关共立案办理食品药品安全领域公益诉讼案件1.5万余件，较去年同期增长12%，食品药品安全领域办案数在公益诉讼办案总数中占比持续提升。最高检要求，继续深入推进“食药安全益路行”检察公益诉讼监督活动，聚焦群众急难愁盼提升办案效果。

  最高检今年初部署开展“食药安全益路行”检察公益诉讼监督活动以来，各地检察机关结合本地实际，精准选取食药安全领域重点突出问题开展深入整治，内容涵盖社区团购食品安全、肉类等食用农产品质量安全等。检察机关针对发现的问题制发检察建议，推动行政机关开展摸底排查和全链条打击，督促整改。

  针对当前食药安全领域仍然存在的问题，最高检要求各地检察机关持续加大食药安全领域公益诉讼办案力度，精准规范办案，强化办案实效，督促行政机关依法履职，开展系统治理。对于食品非法添加、过度医疗等群众反映突出的问题要敢于“动真碰硬”，对整治不到位的问题该诉则诉，突出公益诉讼“诉”的刚性。同时，要强化大数据法律监督模型使用，扩大案件线索来源，提升办案质效。
english_translation:
Xinhua News Agency, Beijing, July 11 (Reporter Liu Shuo) The reporter learned from the Supreme People's Procuratorate on the 11th that in the first half of 2025, the national procuratorates filed more than 15,000 public interest litigation cases in the field of food and drug safety, an increase of 12% over the same period last year. The number of cases in the field of food and drug safety accounted for a continuous increase in the total number of public interest litigation cases. The Supreme People's Procuratorate requires that the "Food and Drug Safety Yiluxing" procuratorial public interest litigation supervision activities be continued and focused on the urgent needs of the people to improve the effectiveness of case handling.

  Since the Supreme People's Procuratorate deployed the "Food and Drug Safety Yiluxing" procuratorial public interest litigation supervision activity at the beginning of this year, local procuratorial organs have combined local realities to accurately select key and prominent issues in the field of food and drug safety for in-depth rectification, covering community group purchase food safety, meat and other edible agricultural product quality safety, etc. The procuratorial organs have issued procuratorial recommendations based on the problems found, promoted administrative organs to conduct a thorough investigation and crackdown on the entire chain, and urged rectification.

  In view of the problems that still exist in the field of food and drug safety, the Supreme People's Procuratorate requires local procuratorates to continue to increase the intensity of public interest litigation in the field of food and drug safety, accurately and standardizedly handle cases, strengthen the effectiveness of case handling, urge administrative agencies to perform their duties in accordance with the law, and carry out systematic governance. We must dare to "take real action" to deal with prominent problems reflected by the masses, such as illegal food additives and excessive medical treatment, and sue those that are not properly rectified, highlighting the rigidity of "litigation" in public interest litigation. At the same time, we must strengthen the use of big data legal supervision models, expand the sources of case clues, and improve the quality and efficiency of case handling.
translation_improvement_points:
1. The term "立案办理" (lì'àn bànlǐ) is translated as "filed." A more comprehensive translation would be "filed and handled" or "formally processed," as the original implies both the registration and subsequent handling of the cases, not just the initial filing.
2. The campaign name "食药安全益路行" (shí yào ānquán yì lù xíng) is transliterated as "Food and Drug Safety Yiluxing." This loses a cultural pun where "益" (yì, meaning 'benefit' or 'public interest') is a homophone for "一" (yī, one). A more descriptive translation like "'Public Interest Journey for Food and Drug Safety' Campaign" would better convey the intended meaning and wordplay.
3. The phrase "群众急难愁盼" (qúnzhòng jí nán chóu pàn) is simplified to "the urgent needs of the people." This is a significant loss of nuance. The original is a four-character idiom referring to issues that are simultaneously urgent, difficult, worrisome, and of great public concern. A better translation would be "the people's most urgent difficulties, pressing concerns, and earnest hopes."
4. The idiom "敢于“动真碰硬”" (gǎnyú “dòng zhēn pèng yìng”) is translated as "dare to 'take real action'." This translation is too mild. The original idiom conveys a sense of bravely tackling difficult, entrenched, and sensitive problems head-on. A more faithful rendering would be "have the courage to tackle tough issues head-on" or "dare to confront entrenched problems."
5. The phrase "该诉则诉" (gāi sù zé sù) is translated as "sue those that are not properly rectified." This translation misses the grammatical structure which implies decisiveness and necessity. A stronger translation would be "to pursue litigation whenever necessary" or "to not hesitate to sue when rectification is inadequate," which better captures the resolute tone.
6. The term "刚性" (gāngxìng) in "“诉”的刚性" is translated as "rigidity." In this legal context, "rigidity" has a slightly negative and inflexible connotation. The original implies a non-negotiable, forceful, and binding power. A better translation would be "the binding force" or "the firm-handed nature" of the litigation component.
7. The common official phrase "结合本地实际" (jiéhé běndì shíjì) is rendered literally as "combined local realities." This is unnatural in English. A more idiomatic translation would be "in accordance with local conditions" or "tailoring efforts to local circumstances."
8. The phrase "加大...办案力度" (jiādà... bàn'àn lìdù) is translated as "increase the intensity of... case handling." A more natural English phrasing would be "to step up efforts in case handling" or "to intensify case handling efforts."
9. The phrase "群众反映突出的问题" (qúnzhòng fǎnyìng túchū de wèntí) is translated as "prominent problems reflected by the masses." This phrasing is clunky. A more fluid and common English equivalent is "issues of strong public concern" or "problems widely reported by the public."
10. The term "督促整改" (dūcù zhěnggǎi) is translated as "urged rectification." The word "督促" (dūcù) implies both supervision and urging. "Urged" alone is too weak. "To oversee and press for rectification" or "to push for corrective measures" would better capture the proactive oversight role.
11. The term "系统治理" (xìtǒng zhìlǐ) is translated as "systematic governance," which is very abstract. In the context of solving specific problems, a more concrete translation like "systemic management" or "to carry out comprehensive, system-wide rectification" would be clearer.
12. The term "深入整治" (shēnrù zhěngzhì) is translated as "in-depth rectification." The word "整治" (zhěngzhì) carries a stronger meaning of cleaning up or cracking down. A translation like "conduct thorough crackdowns" or "carry out in-depth clean-up campaigns" would better reflect the forceful nature of the actions.

original_text:
新华社南宁7月9日电　2025年7月9日，广西壮族自治区柳州市中级人民法院一审公开宣判新疆维吾尔自治区政协原党组成员、副主席窦万贵受贿案，对被告人窦万贵以受贿罪判处死刑，缓期二年执行，剥夺政治权利终身，并处没收个人全部财产；对其受贿所得财物及孳息依法上缴国库，不足部分，继续追缴。



现场图。（广西壮族自治区柳州市中级人民法院供图）

  经审理查明：2003年至2022年，被告人窦万贵利用担任新疆维吾尔自治区托里县委书记，吐鲁番地委委员、组织部部长，阿克苏地委委员、组织部部长，阿克苏地委副书记、政法委书记，阿克苏地委代理书记、书记，新疆维吾尔自治区政协副主席、阿克苏地委书记等职务上的便利，为有关单位和个人在项目承揽、矿产开发、企业经营、职务提拔调整等事项上谋取利益，直接或通过他人非法收受财物共计折合人民币2.29亿余元。

  柳州市中级人民法院认为，被告人窦万贵的行为构成受贿罪，受贿数额特别巨大，并使国家和人民利益遭受特别重大损失，论罪应当判处死刑。鉴于其受贿犯罪中有未遂情节，归案后如实供述自己罪行，主动交代监察机关尚未掌握的绝大部分犯罪事实，认罪悔罪，积极退赃，受贿所得财物及孳息绝大部分已追缴到案，具有法定、酌定从轻处罚情节，对其判处死刑，可不立即执行。法庭遂作出上述判决。
english_translation:
Nanning, July 9 (Xinhua) On July 9, 2025, the Liuzhou Intermediate People's Court of the Guangxi Zhuang Autonomous Region publicly pronounced the first-instance verdict on the bribery case of Dou Wangui, former member of the Party Leadership Group and Vice Chairman of the Xinjiang Uygur Autonomous Region CPPCC. The defendant Dou Wangui was sentenced to death for bribery with a two-year reprieve, deprivation of political rights for life, and confiscation of all personal property; the property and interest obtained from his bribery were turned over to the state treasury in accordance with the law, and the shortfall will continue to be recovered.



On-site photo. (Photo provided by Liuzhou Intermediate People's Court of Guangxi Zhuang Autonomous Region)

  After trial, it was found that from 2003 to 2022, the defendant Dou Wangui used the convenience of his positions as Secretary of the Tori County Party Committee of the Xinjiang Uygur Autonomous Region, member of the Turpan Prefectural Party Committee and Minister of the Organization Department, member of the Aksu Prefectural Party Committee and Minister of the Organization Department, Deputy Secretary and Secretary of the Political and Legal Affairs Commission of the Aksu Prefectural Party Committee, Acting Secretary and Secretary of the Aksu Prefectural Party Committee, Vice Chairman of the Xinjiang Uygur Autonomous Region CPPCC and Secretary of the Aksu Prefectural Party Committee to seek benefits for relevant units and individuals in project contracting, mineral development, business operations, promotion and adjustment of positions, etc., and illegally accepted property directly or through others totaling more than RMB 229 million.

  The Liuzhou Intermediate People's Court held that the defendant Dou Wangui's behavior constituted the crime of accepting bribes, the amount of bribes was extremely huge, and the interests of the state and the people suffered extremely serious losses, and he should be sentenced to death. In view of the fact that his bribery crime was attempted, he truthfully confessed his crime after being arrested, took the initiative to explain most of the criminal facts that the supervisory authority had not yet grasped, pleaded guilty and repented, actively returned the stolen money, and most of the money and interest obtained from the bribes had been recovered, and he had statutory and discretionary mitigating circumstances, and the death penalty was sentenced to him, but it could not be executed immediately. The court then made the above judgment.
translation_improvement_points:
1. The phrase "职务上的便利" (zhíwù shàng de biànlì) is translated as "the convenience of his positions." A more accurate translation reflecting the abuse of power would be "took advantage of his positions" or "abused his authority."
2. The term "谋取利益" (móuqǔ lìyì), translated as "to seek benefits," could be more forcefully rendered as "to secure illicit advantages" or "to obtain improper benefits" to better capture the corrupt nature of the act.
3. The legal term "论罪应当判处死刑" (lùnzuì yīngdāng pànchǔ sǐxíng) is translated as "he should be sentenced to death." This loses the nuance of "论罪" (lùnzuì), which means "judging by the crime itself." A more faithful translation would be "the gravity of his crimes warranted the death penalty."
4. The phrase "受贿数额特别巨大" (shòuhuì shù'é tèbié jùdà) is translated as "the amount of bribes was extremely huge." The term "特别巨大" is a specific legal classification in Chinese criminal law. A more formal rendering would be "the amount of bribes was particularly enormous," reflecting its status as a legal term of art.
5. The clause "使国家和人民利益遭受特别重大损失" (shǐ guójiā hé rénmín lìyì zāoshòu tèbié zhòngdà sǔnshī) is translated passively. A more accurate, active-voice translation would be "caused particularly grave losses to the interests of the state and the people," which better reflects the original's structure and the defendant's culpability.
6. The mitigating factor "受贿犯罪中有未遂情节" (shòuhuì fànzuì zhōng yǒu wèisuì qíngjié) is translated as "his bribery crime was attempted," which is incorrect. It means that among his many crimes, some were attempts. A more precise translation is "the fact that his bribery offenses included instances of attempt."
